# SRC015 - The Persuasion Knowledge Model

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Buyer Psychology |
| Authors / source | Friestad & Wright |
| Year | 1994 |
| Source type | Peer-reviewed theory |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://academic.oup.com/jcr/article/21/1/1/1853712](https://academic.oup.com/jcr/article/21/1/1/1853712) |

## Bias / limitation

Foundational theory; older.

## Core takeaway

People develop knowledge about persuasion attempts and use it to cope with marketers.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use when designing ads for skeptical buyers: do not ask for blind belief; route to verification.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC015
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
